place: Hoan Kiem Lake & Ngoc Son Temple
note: The lakeside promenade is flat and largely step-free for a pleasant circuit, but the temple is reached across the iconic red arched bridge which is stepped. Lakeside easy; temple bridge stepped.

The historic trading maze is flat but the footpaths are narrow, broken and blocked by motorbikes and stalls, forcing you into the busy road; very difficult for wheels and strollers. Chaotic but characterful.
The vast plaza and gardens of the complex are flat and largely step-free to move around; the mausoleum interior has steps and strict rules. Grounds accessible; interior stepped and regimented.
Vietnam's first university is a series of flat courtyards but separated by raised thresholds and a few steps between them; partly accessible across the ground-level courtyards with some obstacles.
A modern museum with ramps, lifts and step-free galleries; the outdoor section has some uneven paths but the main building is accessible. One of the easier sights.
The theatre has accessible seating and a largely step-free entrance for the famous water-puppet show; a seated, accessible cultural experience. Confirm seating when booking.
The historic prison museum is largely at ground level with some accessible sections, but has thresholds and a few steps between areas. Partly accessible.
Hanoi's oldest pagoda on a West Lake islet is reached by a flat causeway (accessible) but the pagoda terraces themselves have steps; view and grounds partly accessible.
The lakeside road and promenade areas are flat and largely step-free, leafier and calmer than the Old Quarter; pleasant for wheeling and strolling.
Wide, flat boulevards with better (if still imperfect) footpaths and grand colonial architecture; the Opera House exterior is viewed from a flat plaza. Easier strolling than the Old Quarter.
The city's largest covered market is flat but densely packed with tight aisles, thresholds and crowds; hard to navigate with wheels or a stroller.
